tire family and the RJD's political image. As soon as Rohini left

word of support from Tejashwi, Lalu Yadav, or Rabri Devi
emerged. This has further heightened the tension. On the one hand,
the party has suffered a crushing defeat in the elections, and on
the other, the legal sword of the 'Land for Jobs' case hangs over
the leadership. These three crises have combined to put the Lalu
family under unprecedented pressure. The question now is how
far this feud will go? Could there be a split within the family? The
question is, will the legal front also prove to be a major factor after
the political loss? It's worth noting that the RJD has already suf-
fered a major defeat in the 2025 Bihar elections.  Tejashwi Yadav,

position in the opposition after being reduced to 25 seats. Ques-
tions are being raised about Tejashwi within the party itself, and

feud, Tej Pratap's displeasure, the alleged silent rivalry between
Misa and Tejashwi, and now Rohini's outburst—all of these have
directly impacted the RJD's credibility. Following the Bihar elec-
tion defeat, the debate over who is to blame is raging, and many
fingers are being pointed at Tejashwi's advisors—Sanjay Yadav
and IT cell in-charge Rameez Nemat Khan. Rohini has also lev-
eled accusations against these same names. Clearly, these ques-
tions and conflicts are not mere rhetoric.  They are a clear indi-
cation that the RJD leadership is in crisis, and the fam-
ily feud has now spilled into the open. The BJP is view-
ing this as an opportunity in a crisis. Tej Pratap's sud-
den grant of "Y" category security is being seen as part
of this political game.  Of the nine children in the Lalu
family, four are active in politics: Misa Bharti, Rohini
Acharya, Tejashwi Yadav, and Tej Pratap Yadav. Politi-
cal analysts believe that each of them has their own am-
bitions, and this is the root of the conflict. Tej Pratap
had already become disgruntled with the party and fam-
ily. Reports of a rift between Misa and Tejashwi over
campaigning are old. Rohini had previously been un-
easy about her political role and the ticket for her son,
and now this dispute has become public.
